text:
Once upon a time, there was a timid squirrel named Sparkle. She lived in a quiet wood nearby a bustling forest teeming with life. Sparkle loved exploring and collecting acorns. But she was scared—more scared than ever.

One day, while hiding behind a big oak tree, she overheard two wise old owls, Olive and Willow, conversing.

Olive hooted pensively. “Every year, little ones claim the woods are quieter than usual. But the truth is, only you feel that; we owls soar above the bustle.”

Willow sighed dreamily. “Perhaps we are just older. Still, there’s something lacking…”

Sparkle knew the owls were talking about her. They noticed her small feet scurrying silently after her beloved acorns. Feeling brave one day, she decided to confront them.

“What do you mean?” she piped up nervously.
